vineyard: Higher elevation wine growing areas below the Mont Sainte-Victoire, foothills of the Maure Mountain range
vinification: Gentle direct pressing and partial liquid maceration (stabulation), before temperature controlled fermentation. The wines are protected with inert gas throughout the winemaking process.
tasting_notes: Very pale pink in color, with peachy reflections. On the nose, an instant burst of white peach, pear, zesty grapefruit and pomelo. Powerful but refined thiol components, which are carefully balanced. On the palate, small red berries precede vibrant citrus notes. Bright and mouthwatering with a lasting, mineral finish.
aroma: White peach, pear, zesty grapefruit and pomelo, thiol components
palate: Small red berries, vibrant citrus notes
food_pairing: Tuna tataki, grilled scallops, burrata and citrus salad with grilled fennel, aperitif with salty nibbles
all_facts: Lovingly referred to as ‘Provence in a Glass’, Mirabeau's flagship rosé wine was inspired by long, languid days on the Côte d’Azur. Sourced from the famed higher elevation wine growing areas below the Mont Sainte-Victoire, bringing the minerality and freshness typical of this microclimate with its poor limestone soils and low night time temperatures. Complementary blending elements are added from the ‘Golden Triangle’ at the foothills of the Maure Mountain range. Night harvested followed by gentle direct pressing. Average Wine Age: 20 to 45 years. Winemaker: Guillaume Cordonis. Estate founded: 2010. Elevation: 328 - 1312 feet.

vinification: Gentle press and free run juices, short maceration, chilled settling and temperature controlled fermentation
aging: Unoaked, released young
tasting_notes: An attractive pale apricot color. On the nose, bursting with notes of red fruits, raspberries and pomegranate, underpinned by a delicate hint of peppery spice. A round and fruitful taste profile, with white cherry and pomegranate, leading to a fine citrus fresh finish.
aroma: Red fruits, raspberries and pomegranate, underpinned by a delicate hint of peppery spice
palate: White cherry and pomegranate
food_pairing: Perfect with roasted Mediterranean vegetables, grilled fish or meat and salads. A spicy tofu and shrimp ramen or slow-cooked pork taco would be a delicious aromatic pairing.
all_facts: Mirabeau Forever Summer Rosé 2024 is a rosé wine from Provence, France. It has an alcohol content of 12.5% and a residual sugar level of less than 1 g/L. The winemaker is Guillaume Cordonis. The estate was founded in 2010 by Stephen & Jeany Cronk.

soil: Helvetian, with sand, clay and calcareous marl
vinification: Traditional method of long maceration: natural fermentation without added yeasts for up to 40 days in stainless steel tanks
aging: 6 months in stainless steel tanks, 30 months in large capacity Slavonian oak casks (25-35 hl) and successive refinement in the bottle
tasting_notes: Deep ruby red color with lively, bright highlights softened by slight traces of purple. It has an intense, ethereal bouquet, with the bitterish flavor of liquorice, withered roses and tar alongside heady, warm and spicy aromas.
aroma: Intense, ethereal, with a bitterish hint of licorice, withered rose and tar, alongside heady, warm and spicy aromas
palate: Full-bodied and austere, with robust tannins and deep notes of dark fruit, earth, and spice.
food_pairing: Recommended with meat, game, and aged cheeses typical of the local cuisine, but also highly suited for all rich dishes of international cuisine.
all_facts: Grape: 100% Nebbiolo (Michet and Lampia varieties). Vineyard Area: 1 Hectare. Exposure: East. Altitude: Hilly zone at around 350 meters a.s.l. Soil: Helvetian, with sand, clay and calcareous marl. Average age of the vines: 20 years. Grape yield per hectare: 80 Quintals. Production: 7,000 bottles a year.

tasting_notes: Characterised by roundness, harmony and fruitiness. The Merlots give a lovely fruitiness and some sweetness, while the Cabernet Sauvignons bring concentration and the Petit Verdots a spicy touch.
aroma: Dark berries, cassis, plum, with hints of cedar and subtle baking spices.
palate: Well-balanced with ripe tannins, concentrated dark fruit, and a smooth, round texture.
food_pairing: Roasted lamb, beef tenderloin, hard cheeses.
all_facts: The mild and rainy winter of 2018-2019 allowed precious groundwater reserves. The summer of 2019 will be remembered as one of the hottest and driest summers since 1900. It offered ideal weather conditions for a homogeneous veraison. Yields: 55hl/ha.

vineyard: Old Vine (Planted 1985)
vinification: Spontaneous fermentation and 100% malolactic fermentation. Pre-cooled and gently whole-bunch pressed.
aging: 11 months in 500L French oak barrels, eleven months in 2000L Stockinger foudres, four months on the lees
tasting_notes: The scent of gold; earthy, mineral yet charged with sun. Powerful, complex aromatics unwind; ripe citrus, fresh honey, orchard apples and pears, all pour forth with bay-leaf, lemon-buchu highlights. Underpinning this, roasted nuts, a line of cream. A concentrated yet fresh core of apple, pear, lime, is wrapped in silky ribbons of golden citrus tapering nutty and honeyed to the edges. The long line of liquid minerality weaves right through for a stony, vibrant finish, echoing with citrus.
aroma: Earthy, mineral yet charged with sun. Ripe citrus, fresh honey, orchard apples and pears, bay-leaf, lemon-buchu highlights. Roasted nuts, a line of cream.
palate: A concentrated yet fresh core of apple, pear, lime, wrapped in silky ribbons of golden citrus tapering nutty and honeyed.
finish: Stony, vibrant finish, echoing with citrus
all_facts: Chenin Blanc from old vine (1985) grown on decomposed shale and granite in Paarl, South Africa. Minimal winemaking employed. Hand-picked at 8 different stages.

vineyard: Lignorelles
tasting_notes: Crisp green apple, lemon zest, and characteristic Chablis minerality. A pure expression of Chardonnay without oak influence, offering a refreshing and tense palate.
aroma: Citrus blossom, green apple, wet stone, and flint.
palate: Zippy acidity, lean and precise with vivid citrus and mineral notes.
food_pairing: Fresh oysters, shellfish, goat cheese.
all_facts: Domaine de la Genillotte has been producing Chablis since 1972. The family behind it, the Depuydts, remain fiercely dedicated to the traditional side of the region. They have never experimented with oak, shun Premier and Grand Cru vineyards and aim to show the purest expression of its soils. Located in the village of Lignorelles in the north-west of Chablis.

vineyard: Côtes des Blancs and Montagne de Reims
vinification: Stainless steel fermenters, full malolactic conversion, Methode Champenoise
aging: Slowly matured in their bottles in underground chalk cellars
tasting_notes: Fresh fruits, citrus and exotic fruits, jasmine, white peaches and pink peppercorns, radiant balance between roundness and smoothness.
aroma: Fresh fruits, with dominant notes of citrus and exotic fruits, followed by a touch of jasmine, white peaches and pink peppercorns.
palate: Precise and clean, as the wine delivers a radiant balance between roundness and smoothness.
all_facts: Champagne Ruinart Blanc de Blancs NV is a 100% Chardonnay Champagne made from grapes grown in Premier Cru vineyards. The wine has been blended from various years, with 20 to 25% having been reserved from the previous two vintages. Vegan and milk-free.

tasting_notes: This is an atypical blend for Chateau Beaumont 2021, which brings a nice freshness, fruit and silky tannins to the wine. The Merlots have very nice ripe fruit, the Cabernet Sauvignons are powerful with fine tannins.
aroma: Red fruits, subtle spice, and a touch of earthiness.
palate: Fresh and fruity with silky tannins.
food_pairing: Grilled meats, stews, and hard cheeses.
all_facts: Vintage 2021, atypical blend: Merlot (65%), Cabernet Sauvignon (30%), Petit Verdot (5%). Yields 40 hl/ha.

vineyard: Gualtallary, Tupungato
vinification: Indigenous yeasts, maximum temperature of 29°C (84°F) for 20 days, followed by a 15-day infusion with minimal movement.
aging: 15 months in foudre (50%), barrel (25%), tank (25%).
tasting_notes: Rich and concentrated, with intense aromas of black fruit, cherry, and dark chocolate. The palate offers robust tannins, excellent structure, and a long, elegant finish with hints of spice and minerality.
aroma: Blackberries, dark cherries, plum, violet, and subtle oak spices.
palate: Full-bodied with a great mineral core and fine-grained tannins.
food_pairing: Grilled steak, empanadas, roasted root vegetables.
all_facts: Malbec and Cabernet Franc blend (90% and 10% respectively). Altitude: 1470 m / 4822 ft. Winemaker: Alejandro Vigil. 2022 was considered by Vigil as one of the best vintages he has experienced.

vineyard: Quinta da Fonte Souto
harvest: August 16th with Verdelho, followed by Arinto
vinification: Fermentation takes place in stainless steel vats at low temperatures.
aging: Four months in stainless steel vats with minimal contact with oxygen.
tasting_notes: Broad and lifted aromatic expression, ranging from floral orange blossom and honeysuckle scents to pineapple, mango, and citrus notes. On the palate, the lively acidity provides a stimulating counterpoint to the ample, creamy fruit range, giving the wine exquisite elegance and balance.
aroma: Floral orange blossom and honeysuckle scents to pineapple, mango, and citrus notes.
palate: Lively acidity provides a stimulating counterpoint to the ample, creamy fruit range.
all_facts: The 207-hectare Quinta da Fonte Souto was acquired by the Symington family in 2017. Lying on the slopes of the São Mamede Range at an altitude of 500 metres. Harvesting of the white grapes began in early September.

soil: Clay and sandy type of soil
vineyard: Different vineyards in the valley
vinification: Vinified for 7 days in whole bunches with wild yeast in Inox tanks.
aging: 6 months in Inox tanks.
tasting_notes: Medium-bodied, with silky red fruit flavors, deep spice and hints of licorice. A wine of equal parts ease and elegance, rugged and refined, and woven with a web of fine-grained tannins and a lingering mineral finish.
aroma: Enticing aromas of crunchy cherry and blackberry lead into an herbal yet ethereal brilliance that refuses to be pinned down.
palate: Silky red fruit flavors, deep spice, and fine-grained tannins.
food_pairing: Mediterranean fare, grilled vegetables, tapas.
all_facts: 100% Grenache Noir, fruit-driven, medium-bodied, produced by Terroir Sense Fronteres.

aging: 5 years in seasoned oak casks
tasting_notes: Opulent, concentrated, and beautifully balanced, with rich dark fruit flavors, chocolate notes, and a structured finish.
aroma: Dark berries, black plum, cocoa, and subtle spices.
palate: Rich and luscious, packed with ripe black fruit flavors.
food_pairing: Dark chocolate desserts or hard cheeses.
all_facts: Graham's LBV Port 2019 was matured for 5 years in Graham's 1890 port lodge. The 2019 vintage was a fabulous year for making port due to tiny crops leading to intense, concentrated wines. It should be consumed within six weeks of opening.

vineyard: Quinta dos Malvedos, Quinta do Tua, Quinta das Lages, Quinta da Vila Velha, Quinta do Vale de Malhadas
vinification: Matured in seasoned oak casks until their peak of maturity is reached.
aging: Average 20 Years Old
tasting_notes: On the nose, it shows an excellent bouquet with a characteristic ‘nutty’ character and delicious mature fruit with hints of orange peel, exquisitely mellowed by careful ageing. On the palate, it is rich, softly sweet and smooth, perfectly balanced, with a long and elegant finish.
aroma: Nutty, mature fruit, orange peel.
palate: Rich, softly sweet, smooth, perfectly balanced.
food_pairing: Nuts, dried fruits and fruitcake, vanilla ice cream or crème brûlée.
all_facts: Graham’s 20 Year Old Tawny captures the perfect balance between young and aged Port wines. It is made from the finest quality wines matured in seasoned oak casks.

vineyard: 200 hectares
vinification: Temperature-controlled stainless steel vats on fine lees
tasting_notes: This is a delicious, thirst-quenching rose – delicate and elegant. Pale in color, it is light, crisp and zesty, offering flavors of citrus, pink grapefruit, peach and raspberry with a long and refreshing finish.
aroma: Citrus zest, fresh berries, and light floral notes.
palate: Light, crisp and zesty, offering flavors of citrus, pink grapefruit, peach and raspberry.
food_pairing: Ideal as an aperitif; pairs with light fare, Provencal dishes, grilled seafood, salads and Pan-Asian cuisine.
all_facts: Château La Coste is a winery, art center, and resort in Southern France, about 20 kilometers north of Aix-en-Provence. The estate we see today originated in 2003, when Irish art collector Patrick McKillen bought the property. Uses organic processes and applies biodynamic principles.

vineyard: 29 crus
aging: At least 3 years of ageing
tasting_notes: Ample, straightforward, round with a long, persistent freshness. Beautiful notes of fresh citrus, stone fruits, and toasted brioche.
aroma: Generous and salivating notes of cake with fresh citrus fruits, the exuberance of smoke evolving into pure chalk, not forgetting to mention the basket of stone fruits and red fruits.
palate: Ample, straightforward, round with a long, persistent freshness.
all_facts: Henriot Brut Souverain is a multi-cru, multi-vintage blend created by Apolline Henriot in 1808. The blend consists of approximately 45% Pinot Noir, 40% Chardonnay and 15% Meunier. 50 to 70% of wines of the year and 30 to 50% of reserve wines are used.

vineyard: Clos Uroulat
vinification: Slow and gentle pressing. Fermentation & aging 10 months in barrels (20% new barrels on average).
tasting_notes: A lusciously sweet wine with vibrant acidity. Flavors of candied citrus, ripe mango, and honey are perfectly balanced.
aroma: Tropical fruits, honey, and subtle toasted oak.
palate: Rich and sweet but well-balanced by brilliant acidity.
food_pairing: Foie gras, savory pies, cheeses, fruit desserts, pancakes, galettes des rois, pudding.
all_facts: Wine from the Jurançon appellation. Manual harvest in November. Slow and gentle pressing, fermentation and aging in oak barrels. Highly regarded as an aperitif or dessert wine.

vineyard: La Roche Vineuse
vinification: Temperature-controlled stainless-steel vats, using only indigenous yeasts, malolactic conversion.
aging: Around ten months in tank on fine lees.
tasting_notes: Bright yellow in colour, with lively citrus, ripe apple and fragrant white flower aromas on the nose. The palate is medium-bodied, generous and round with beautifully rich stone fruit flavours that are tempered by a fresh, zippy acidity.
aroma: Citrus, ripe apple, fragrant white flower.
palate: Medium-bodied, generous and round with beautifully rich stone fruit flavours that are tempered by a fresh, zippy acidity.
food_pairing: Roast chicken, grilled white fish, creamy pasta dishes.
all_facts: In 2005, Jean-Christophe Perraud founded Domaine Perraud. Fruit comes from organically-farmed vineyards across 15 different plots in the commune of La Roche Vineuse. The vines are on average 25 years old.

vineyard: South coast of the island between Ribeira Brava and Calheta
vinification: Fermented with natural yeasts at 18°C to 21°C. Fortified with grape spirit (96% v/v) to arrest fermentation at the desired degree of sweetness.
aging: Aged for an average of 10 years in oak casks under the traditional 'Canteiro' system.
tasting_notes: Medium sweet, subtly completed by caramel and vanilla. On the palate, it is sumptuous and rich with a persistent finish.
aroma: Dried fruits, caramel, vanilla, and warm spices.
palate: Sumptuous, medium sweet, perfectly balancing richness with tangy acidity.
food_pairing: Fruitcake, dark chocolate, blue cheese.
all_facts: Blandy’s Madeira Wine has over two centuries and seven generations of history. They are the only family of all the original founders of the Madeira wine trade that still owns and manages their company. Bual produces a medium-rich style of Madeira.

vineyard: Castiglione Tinella, Santo Stefano Belbo, Castagnole Lanze, Calosso
vinification: The must is kept in stainless steel containers at -3°C. When there is market demand, it is transferred into autoclaves for temperature-controlled fermentation, microfiltered and bottled.
tasting_notes: Intense, elegant and lingering. The sweet sensations are well balanced with a pleasant acidity and fruity aromas typical of Muscat grapes.
aroma: Orange blossom, peach and thyme.
palate: Sweet, vibrant, and elegant, balanced with refreshing acidity.
food_pairing: Fruit tarts, panettone, spicy Asian cuisine.
all_facts: Moscato d’Asti is an indigenous aromatic wine characterized by orange, peach and lime notes. The wine has a straw yellow color with greenish hues and fine perlage.

vineyard: Around Menfi on the south-western coast of Sicily
vinification: Fermented in stainless-steel tanks at 22-25°C with six to eight days of skin contact.
aging: Three to five months in stainless-steel tanks.
tasting_notes: This wine is medium ruby red in colour with purple hints. On the nose, it has aromas of black cherries, plums and red berries. The palate is velvety with intense fruit flavours of plums and red berries.
aroma: Black cherries, plums and red berries.
palate: Velvety with intense fruit flavours of plums and red berries.
food_pairing: Pasta dishes with meat sauces, grilled vegetables, medium-aged cheeses.
all_facts: Vineyards are Guyot trained and planted at a density of 4,000-4,500 plants per hectare. Winemaker Alberto Antonini has sought to improve viticulture alongside Pedro Parra and Mimmo De Gregorio.

vineyard: Nine vineyards in Sussex, Hampshire and Kent
vinification: Coquard presses, stainless steel, malolactic fermentation, blended with reserve wines
aging: 36 months on lees, 6 months after disgorgement
tasting_notes: Toasty, spicy and complex aromas showing wonderful development after extended ageing. The palate supports these complex aromas with honey, almond, pastry and baked apple flavours. Very fine and elegant.
aroma: Toasty, spicy and complex, showing wonderful development.
palate: Honey, almond, pastry and baked apple flavours. Intense and delicate.
food_pairing: Smoked salmon, oysters, poultry.
all_facts: Nyetimber crafts wines exclusively from estate-grown grapes using the traditional method.

vineyard: Estate vineyards on the north side of the Downs
vinification: Coquard Presses, stainless-steel tanks. Second fermentation took place in bottle. Dosage: 44g/l (historically or rounded off to 38g/L).
aging: Five years on lees before disgorging.
tasting_notes: A gastronomic wine, this pairs brilliantly with sweet and fragrant savoury dishes alike. Light golden hues illuminate this delicately effervescent wine. Aromas of brioche, baked apple and Acacia honey are followed by a hint of tangerine peel. The palate of lemon curd is balanced by crisp acidity.
aroma: Brioche, baked apple, Acacia honey, tangerine peel.
palate: Lemon curd, crisp acidity, clean, pure structure.
food_pairing: Sweet and fragrant savoury dishes, Asian cuisine, light desserts.
all_facts: Its name is a reference to Nyetimber’s skilled winemaker, Cherie Spriggs, as well as its higher level of dosage, classifying it as a Demi-Sec style.

tasting_notes: Attractive bouquet with youthful, delicate, creamy hints with a touch of biscuity and yeasty notes. Lively persistent bubbles with clean, soft, creamy fruit, excellent intensity and a lingering finish. Elegant and balanced.
aroma: Biscuity and yeasty notes, floral undertones.
palate: Clean, soft, creamy fruit, excellent intensity.
food_pairing: White fish, light poultry, vegetable terrines.
all_facts: Founded in Epernay in 1811, Perrier-Jouët owns 108 hectares of vineyards. The blend is typically 20% Chardonnay, 40% Pinot Noir, and 40% Pinot Meunier.

vineyard: 137-hectare estate, predominantly north and west facing
vinification: Hand-picked, manual sorting, gravity-fed fermentation in small tanks.
aging: 9 months in French oak barrels (400 and 225 litres).
tasting_notes: Well-balanced wine with expressive fruit and harmonious flavours, ready to enjoy young whilst conserving good ageing potential.
aroma: Vibrant red and black fruits, floral hints, and subtle oak spice.
palate: Rich, expressive fruit, well-integrated tannins, and elegant structure.
food_pairing: Hearty meat dishes, stews. Compatible with vegetarian and vegan diets.
all_facts: The wine will benefit from some cellaring and will develop favourably in bottle through 2030. Bottled in April 2023.

vineyard: Near the hamlet of Blagny, upper part of Puligny-Montrachet
vinification: Hand-harvested, lightly crushed, pneumatic press.
aging: 12 months on fine lees in oak barrels (20% new), then 6 months in stainless vat.
tasting_notes: A limpid and brilliant appearance. Firm and masculine on the palate, carrying fresh and discreet scents typical of high-altitude Puligny.
aroma: White flowers, citrus, subtle almond, and strong minerality.
palate: Firm, structured, and vibrant with deep mineral tension.
food_pairing: Sushi, grilled white fish, poultry in cream sauce.
all_facts: Vines were planted across multiple years: 1952, 1964, 1969, 2002, and 2004. Total production is roughly 6,000 bottles annually.

soil: Taunus quartzite with an overlay of fertile loam and loess, small amount of iron dioxide.
vineyard: 50-hectare single vineyard - Schloss Johannisberg
harvest: Selective hand harvest at the end of September
vinification: Fermentation: 80% stainless steel, 20% wooden cask (1200L made from estate oaks).
tasting_notes: Pronounced aromas of yellow fruit, such as juicy vineyard peach, apricot and yellow apple, with a hint of rosemary and cool mint. A fresh, grippy acidity and balanced fruit sweetness on the palate.
aroma: Yellow fruit (peach, apricot, apple), rosemary, and cool mint.
palate: Fresh, grippy acidity, balanced fruit sweetness, mellow with a slight melt.
finish: Herbal notes and a hint of grapefruit.
food_pairing: Fish dishes, poultry, vegetables, taboulé with mint.
all_facts: 2024 was a rainy year necessitating a highly selective hand harvest. Grapes possessed a balanced, lively acidity structure.

vineyard: Côte des Blancs, Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne
vinification: Fermentation in temperature-regulated stainless steel fermenters, short maceration, full malolactic fermentation.
tasting_notes: Delicate pomegranate pink with slightly orange reflections. The attack is distinct and full, with gentle effervescence and aromas of freshly picked berries.
aroma: Tropical fruits (guava and lychee), small berries (raspberries, cherries and wild strawberries), rose and pomegranate notes.
palate: Aromas of freshly picked berries; balance brings together a delightful freshness and voluptuous body.
finish: Elegant bracing touch of mint and pink grapefruit.
food_pairing: Italian prosciutto, mi-cuit salmon, leg of milk-fed lamb.
all_facts: Ruinart Rosé is a festive champagne blend of Chardonnay and Pinot Noir, primarily from Premiers Crus vineyards. Known as one of the first rosé champagnes ever produced.

vinification: Traditional harvest, fermented in small steel vats.
tasting_notes: Yellow dress with gold tints. Aromas of candied apricot, peach, mango and toasty notes. On the palate, the attack is vivacious. The wine is perfectly balanced between freshness and botrytis.
aroma: Candied apricot, peach, mango and toasty notes.
palate: Vivacious attack, luscious sweetness balanced perfectly with fresh acidity.
food_pairing: Chicken liver paté, veal with mushroom risotto, blue cheese.
all_facts: Blend of top cuvées from Sauternes and Barsac domains. 20 years average vine age. Blend is typically 90% Sémillon, 10% Sauvignon Blanc.

tasting_notes: Rich, honeyed, and luscious with intense flavors of dried apricots, orange marmalade, figs, and exotic spices. Perfectly balanced by a vibrant, zesty acidity that provides a long, elegant finish.
aroma: Dried apricot, honey, orange peel, botrytis, and subtle spice.
palate: Lusciously sweet, full-bodied, yet completely refreshed by its piercing acidity.
food_pairing: Foie gras, blue cheeses like Roquefort, fruit tarts, or enjoyed on its own as a dessert.
all_facts: 6 Puttonyos indicates a very high level of residual sugar and concentration, made from individually hand-picked botrytized grapes (Aszú berries) macerated in a base wine or must.

soil: Deep silt-rich calcareous clay soils, reddish marl formations from the Oligocene period.
vineyard: Milmanda Estate (15 ha / 37 acres, 500 m elevation)
vinification: Fermented in barrels (80%) and foudres (20%) without malolactic fermentation.
aging: Aged for 4 months in 300L barrels and 1500L foudres (50% new French oak), then aged on lees in tanks for 12 months.
tasting_notes: Complex and elegantly structured, showcasing the purity of the terroir and thoughtful oak integration.
aroma: White peach, toasted hazelnut, subtle vanilla, and white flowers.
palate: Rich yet sharply focused, with great textural weight balanced by driving acidity.
food_pairing: Grilled lobster, creamy poultry dishes, soft ripened cheeses.
all_facts: Milmanda was part of a route of medieval castles that sheltered Christians during the period of the Reconquista. The 2021 vintage had a drier spring but beneficial summer rains, creating an exceptional harvest.

vinification: Cold maceration for 24 hours at 8 degrees. Fermentation takes place at a controlled temperature in stainless steel for about 10-15 days.
tasting_notes: Bright and expressive floral aromas follow through on the palate which is concentrated and vibrant with juicy apricot, green apple and pear fruit, and mineral notes.
aroma: Bright and expressive floral aromas.
palate: Concentrated and vibrant with juicy apricot, green apple and pear fruit, and mineral notes.
food_pairing: Seafood appetizers, light pasta dishes, fresh cheeses.
all_facts: A pristine organic representation of the Trebbiano grape, emphasizing freshness and bright fruit characteristics through careful stainless steel vinification.

vineyard: Choice vineyards around La Motte in Provence
harvest: Sunrise to noon; grapes sorted optically
vinification: Grapes sorted optically before vinification.
aging: 3-4 months in stainless steel on lees.
tasting_notes: Very pure, round and flavourful rose with beautiful acidity and body. Fine and elegant aromatic framework with length; aromatic and sharply structured finish.
aroma: Elegant aromatic notes of fresh red fruit and Provence herbs.
palate: Round, flavourful and balanced with light body and lively acidity.
food_pairing: Ideal aperitif; light dishes, Provencal-style cuisine and Pan-Asian cuisine.
all_facts: Range: Whispering Angel. Known globally as a benchmark for Provence Rosé. Allergens: Sulphites. Vegan friendly: No. Vegetarian friendly: No.

tasting_notes: Opening with bright lime zest, white peach and green apple. Developing elegantly into perfumed notes of jasmine, green tea and orange blossom, giving way to a generous finish of fresh green garden notes.
aroma: Lime zest, white peach, green apple, jasmine, green tea, orange blossom.
palate: Crisp and vibrant, showing pure fruit expression without the alcohol weight.
food_pairing: Enjoy in a wide lipped tulip glass for optimum taste. Excellent base for non-alcoholic cocktails or paired with light salads.
all_facts: A premium alcohol-free sparkling wine from Rheinhessen, Germany. Production involves freezing grape juice, filtered water, and sterile filtration rather than dealcoholization, preserving natural aromas.

wine_name: Yalumba Samuel's Collection Eden Valley Viognier
vinification: 60% gently pressed directly into old French oak barriques and puncheons, remainder into stainless steel tanks. Wild yeast fermentation.
aging: 10 months on lees with regular bâtonnage.
tasting_notes: Pure aromas reminiscent of fresh apricots, lifted ginger, saffron and fresh white flowers. The palate is long, rich and luscious, with intense and expressive stone fruit flavours finishing with an alluring freshness.
aroma: Fresh apricots, lifted ginger, saffron and fresh white flowers.
palate: Long, rich and luscious, with intense and expressive stone fruit flavours.
finish: Alluring freshness, hinting at honey and toast complexity over time.
food_pairing: Enjoy with a Moroccan tagine and spiced couscous, or baba ganoush with sautéed saltbush.
all_facts: Robert Hill-Smith is the estate owner, Louisa Rose is the winemaker. The 2022 growing season produced Viognier grapes with pristine fruit flavours. The juice was allowed to passively interact with the air, oxidising out bitter phenolics.
